hypotheses.label: AFLFast FAST n_fuzz rarity damping de-prioritizes a frequently-hit (saturated) seed and starves the deep path of sustained energy; minimizer's calibration-only schedule (PowerSchedule None) keeps allocating mutations and reaches it

params.note: would require per-seed n_fuzz/energy-allocation traces and per-trial depth_reach past the damped seed; neither is emitted by the campaign harness

hypotheses.prediction: IF measurable: fast_energy_share_on_common_edge_seed << minimizer_energy_share_on_common_edge_seed (FAST damps the saturated hash-lookup / attribute-validation bucket to ~0.4x) AND fast_depth_reach < minimizer_depth_reach (naive/uniform energy reaches deeper past the damped seed). An alternative (corpus-content / comparison-solving mechanism) would predict NO energy-share gap and a content signature in the corpus instead.
hypotheses.rule: DECIDABLE:FALSE -- no rule can fire. fast_energy_share_on_common_edge_seed and *_depth_reach are NOT obtainable: this is a runtime scheduling dynamic (campaign-wide n_fuzz history + per-seed energy allocation over time), not derivable from static per-branch corpus content; and the campaign emits no per-seed energy / n_fuzz traces. Compounding: both branches are libxml2, for which NO on-disk corpus or resolving/blocking seeds exist (data_realities: corpora only for curl/harfbuzz/openthread/sqlite3), so even a corpus-content proxy has zero data to score.

notes: Target: libxml2 (both branches, 6414 xmlHashLookup3 / 7146 xmlValidateNmtokensValueInternal). Single discriminable cluster carried over from the prior (aflfast_nfuzz_damping_starves_deep_path, n=2); the two branches share one mechanism (n_fuzz energy damping on a saturated bucket) and are NOT separable from each other, so no split. WHY NON-DISCRIMINABLE (G3, honest inconclusive): (1) The mechanism is a PURE SCHEDULING / energy-allocation dynamic -- FAST multiplies energy by an n_fuzz log2 factor computed from campaign-wide bucket hit-frequency. That is a runtime, per-trial, time-evolving quantity; it leaves NO per-branch fingerprint in saved corpus content (the byte content of seeds is identical-vocabulary across all 4 fuzzers per data_realities; the divergence is in HOW MUCH energy each seed earned, not WHAT bytes it holds). (2) The campaign harness emits no per-seed n_fuzz counts, energy/perf_score, or depth_reach traces, so the proposed exec_stats:energy_share descriptor has no underlying signal to read -- registry_tool:null and unbuildable from current data. (3) data_realities forbids any seed/corpus measurement on libxml2 (on-disk corpora exist only for curl/harfbuzz/openthread/sqlite3), so even a corpus-proxy fallback scores zero branches. Per the contract, an honest 'no clean test' beats a rule the data cannot support: the single hypothesis is marked decidable:false and fallback:inconclusive carries both branches. The Layer-1 resolve pattern (fast BLOCKS / minimizer RESOLVES => aflfast-anti) remains the label-source; G2-independent verification is simply not available for a scheduling-only divergence with this instrumentation and these targets.
